title: Living in Boulder vlog 2 -  26 -  2017: Google Earthing on Circus Maximus Bike Time Trials

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a morning vlog update about his 'bike racer levels' project, which maps historical endurance racing distances (Circus Maximus chariot races, Greek and Byzantine lap counts) onto modern cycling time trial benchmarks using Google Earth. He explains recent adjustments to distance categories (adding 10km, restructuring 5km and 20km tiers) and outlines his goal of building an interactive calculator that factors in age, country, and lifestyle to assign users a fitness level. He also mentions wanting to eventually build a full recreation of ancient Rome in Google Earth.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Finds deep value in connecting ancient racing (Circus Maximus, Greeks, Byzantines) to modern endurance sports as a way to motivate cyclists and appreciate modern technology
Prefers to front-load tedious work so he can end on exciting, rewarding tasks to maintain psychological momentum
Embraces a non-normalized sleep cycle, finding it peaceful and productive to work at night
